task.task_description: This is a systematic electromagnetic calorimetry study investigating the relationship between detector depth and shower containment performance. The task involves simulating electromagnetic showers in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter at three different depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple electron beam energies (1, 5, and 20 GeV). The study requires measuring energy containment fractions, energy resolution, and shower maximum positions to characterize how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower development and leakage. This is a parametric design study that combines detector physics simulation with performance optimization to establish minimum depth requirements for acceptable energy containment.
task.goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV electron energy

constraints.description: Use homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter material only
Energy leakage must be below 1% at 20 GeV
Run one depth configuration per geometry call
Use Geant4 particle gun with electrons

task.context:
Investigate how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower containment.

**STUDY DESIGN:**
Use a homogeneous PbWO4 (lead tungstate) calorimeter block.
Test multiple calorimeter depths by varying the number of layers or total thickness.

Target depths to compare:
- 16 radiation lengths (X₀ of PbWO4 = 0.89 cm, so ~14.2 cm)
- 20 radiation lengths (~17.8 cm)
- 25 radiation lengths (~22.3 cm)

**GEOMETRY OPTIONS:**
- "box" or "homogeneous_slab" topology with PbWO4 as active material
- Vary the active_thickness parameter to set different depths

**PRACTICAL CONSTRAINTS (for tractable simulation):**
- Energy sweep: use particle_energy: [1, 5, 20] (GeV)
- Use e- (electrons) in Geant4 particle gun
- Run one depth configuration per geometry call

**MEASURE at each depth and energy:**
1. Fraction of incident energy deposited in the calorimeter (containment)
2. Energy resolution (σ_E / E)
3. Position of shower maximum (depth of peak energy deposition from hit z-positions)

**GOAL:**
Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV.
Plot containment fraction vs depth for each energy.

Test 3 configurations (one per depth).

steps.name: generate_geometry_16X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for 16 radiation length PbWO4 calorimeter (14.2 cm depth)

steps.name: generate_geometry_20X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for 20 radiation length PbWO4 calorimeter (17.8 cm depth)

steps.name: generate_geometry_25X0
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry for 25 radiation length PbWO4 calorimeter (22.3 cm depth)

steps.name: simulate_shallow_depths
steps.description: Simulate electromagnetic showers in 16X0 and 20X0 PbWO4 calorimeters with electron energies [1, 5, 20] GeV

steps.name: simulate_deep_depth
steps.description: Simulate electromagnetic showers in 25X0 PbWO4 calorimeter with electron energies [1, 5, 20] GeV

steps.name: analyze_containment_performance
steps.description: Analyze energy containment fractions, energy resolution, and shower maximum positions for all depth configurations and energies

steps.name: create_containment_plots
steps.description: Generate publication-quality plots showing containment fraction vs depth for each energy and energy resolution vs depth

steps.name: evaluate_depth_requirement
steps.description: Determine if minimum calorimeter depth for <1% energy leakage at 20 GeV has been identified

steps.name: generate_final_report
steps.description: Synthesize all results into comprehensive report on calorimeter depth optimization with recommendations

plan.reasoning: This workflow systematically investigates electromagnetic shower containment by generating three different calorimeter depths, running two comprehensive simulation steps (staying within the 2-simulation budget), and performing detailed analysis to determine the minimum depth requirement. The approach uses energy sweeps to efficiently test multiple energies in parallel, analyzes containment performance across all configurations, and provides clear visualization of results to identify the optimal calorimeter depth for the specified leakage requirements.

The task involves simulating electromagnetic showers in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter at three different depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple electron beam energies (1, 5, and 20 GeV). The study requires measuring energy containment fractions, energy resolution, and shower maximum positions to characterize how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower development and leakage. This is a parametric design study that combines detector physics simulation with performance optimization to establish minimum depth requirements for acceptable energy containment.
Goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV electron energy
Status: SUCCESS
Progress: 85.0%
Completed: 9/9 steps
Reasoning: GEOMETRY_EXISTS: Three .gdml files present (pbwo4_16x0_calorimeter.gdml, pbwo4_20x0_calorimeter.gdml, pbwo4_25x0_calorimeter.gdml). SIMULATION_RAN: Multiple .root and .parquet files with substantial sizes, 10,000 events per energy point. ANALYSIS_PRODUCED_RESULTS: Quantitative containment fractions (0.919867-0.99022) and energy resolutions (0.010262-0.043364) reported. RESULTS_PHYSICALLY_VALID: All containment fractions in [0,1], energy resolutions positive. GOAL_ADDRESSED: User requested depth vs containment study to keep leakage <1% at 20GeV - analysis identifies minimum depth of 22.3cm achieving 0.99022 containment. REPORT_EXISTS: Final report generated. PHYSICS_QUALITY DEGRADED: Energy resolution reported as individual values (0.026168, 0.031937, 0.043364) but lacks proper calorimeter parameterization sigma_E/E = a/sqrt(E) + b with fit R2.
